---
title: Jenkins
description: CI/CD automation server with Docker-in-Docker support for building and deploying pipelines.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `jenkins/jenkins:lts-jdk21`  
**Ports:** `8080` (web UI / REST API), `50000` (JNLP agent)  
**Data:** `jenkins_home` (named volume)

## Introduction

[Jenkins](https://www.jenkins.io/) is a widely-used open-source CI/CD automation server. This template runs the LTS release with JDK 21 and Docker-in-Docker support, enabling pipelines to build and push Docker images directly.

## When to Use

- You need a self-hosted CI/CD server with pipeline-as-code (Jenkinsfile)
- Your pipelines build Docker images or run Docker containers
- You want integration with SonarQube for code quality gates
- You need a free, open-source Jenkins alternative to hosted CI services

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph LR
    Dev["Developer\ngit push"] --> VCS["Git repository"]
    VCS -->|"webhook"| Jenkins
    subgraph Jenkins["Jenkins Container (privileged + root)"]
        Master["Jenkins Master\n:8080"]
        Pipeline["Pipeline execution"]
        JHome["jenkins_home volume"]
    end
    Master --> Pipeline
    Pipeline -->|"Docker builds\nvia socket"| DockerSocket["/var/run/docker.sock"]
    DockerSocket --> Registry["Docker registry\nor local image"]
    JNLP["External agents\n:50000 JNLP"] --> Master
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- Docker socket available on host (for Docker-in-Docker builds)

## Ports

| Port | Protocol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `8080` | HTTP | Web UI and REST API |
| `50000` | TCP | JNLP agent connections |

Both configurable via `JENKINS_PORT` and `JENKINS_AGENT_PORT`.

:::note[Port conflict]
Port 8080 is also the default for Kafka UI. If running both, set `JENKINS_PORT=8081` in `jenkins/.env`.
:::

## Volumes

| Volume | Contents |
|---|---|
| `jenkins_home` | Jobs, plugins, pipeline workspaces, credentials |

## Configuration

```bash
cd jenkins
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `JENKINS_PORT` | `8080` | Web UI port |
| `JENKINS_AGENT_PORT` | `50000` | JNLP agent port |

## Workflow

```bash
# Start
docker compose up -d

# Get the initial admin password
docker compose logs jenkins | grep -A 5 "Please use the following password"
# OR
docker compose exec jenkins cat /var/jenkins_home/secrets/initialAdminPassword

# Open setup wizard
open http://localhost:8080

# Install suggested plugins, create admin user, complete setup

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove data (jobs, plugins, config — destructive)
docker compose down -v
```

**First-run wizard:**
1. Enter the initial admin password from the logs
2. Choose **Install suggested plugins** (or select manually)
3. Create an admin user
4. Set the Jenkins URL (use your domain if behind NPM)

## Use Cases

**Docker image pipeline:**
```groovy
pipeline {
    agent any
    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t my-app:${BUILD_NUMBER} .'
            }
        }
        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker run --rm my-app:${BUILD_NUMBER} pytest'
            }
        }
    }
}
```

**SonarQube quality gate:**
```groovy
stage('Analyse') {
    steps {
        withSonarQubeEnv('sonarqube') {
            sh 'mvn sonar:sonar'
        }
    }
}
stage('Quality Gate') {
    steps {
        waitForQualityGate abortPipeline: true
    }
}
```

## Security

:::danger[Privileged mode]
This template runs Jenkins as `root` with `privileged: true` to support Docker-in-Docker. This gives the container unrestricted access to the host kernel. **Never expose port 8080 directly to the internet.** Use Nginx Proxy Manager with authentication in front of Jenkins.
:::

:::caution[Docker socket]
The Docker socket mount gives Jenkins (running as root) full control over all containers on the host. Restrict who can trigger builds or access Jenkins.
:::

## Integration

### Nginx Proxy Manager

Uncomment the NPM block in `docker-compose.yml`, restart, and create a proxy host: `jenkins.yourdomain.com → jenkins:8080`.

### SonarQube

In Jenkins: **Manage Jenkins → System → SonarQube servers** → add server URL `http://sonarqube:9000` (when both are on `proxy-net`).

### Portainer

Deploy as a Portainer stack. Set `JENKINS_PORT` if needed.

- [SonarQube Jenkins plugin](https://docs.sonarqube.org/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/jenkins-extension-sonarqube/)
